key_insight: ComBench shows that Olympiad-level combinatorics splits into two distinct skills—rigorous proof reasoning and constructive realization—and top models are still far from mastering both.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark provides a practical diagnostic for judging whether a model can produce mathematically valid arguments and verifiable constructions, which matters for reliable theorem-style reasoning systems.
method: Two tracks: analysis-centric proof problems and construction-centric realization problems
Rubric-guided proof grading plus deterministic construction verification
evidence_signal: Across frontier models, the strongest system reaches 65.4% overall Avg. and 75.3% overall Best@4, and the abstract reports that proof quality and construction validity can diverge.
watch_item: Construction tasks are especially brittle because a model may present a plausible idea without producing a fully valid explicit construction or a verifiable proof of correctness.
paper_title: ComBench: A Benchmark for Rigorous Proof Reasoning and Constructive Realization in Olympiad-Level Combinatorics
